page.title: Marbella Sunrise Fishing Charter: Catch the Dawn Catch

data.meta_description: Book a sunrise fishing charter Marbella and reel in the dawn catch from Puerto Banús. Licensed skipper, gear, drinks & snacks included. 5-12 pax.
data.summary: Our Marbella sunrise fishing charter departs Puerto Banús at first light for a 4-hour trip targeting local species. Includes licensed skipper, all fishing gear, drinks, and snacks for up to 12 guests.
key_facts: Departs Puerto Banús at dawn (approx. 06:30–07:00 depending on season)
4-hour charter: €749 on Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax)
All fishing gear, bait, and tackle provided – just bring your hat
Licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%) included
Catch: sea bass, bream, mackerel, and occasional dentex
Soft drinks, water, beer, cava, white wine, and light snacks on board
Catch-and-release encouraged; keep what you'll eat
Private charter – your group only, no strangers

faq.q: What time does a sunrise fishing charter in Marbella depart?
faq.a: Departure is at first light, typically between 06:30 and 07:00 depending on the season. We meet at Puerto Banús 15 minutes before. The exact time is confirmed the day before based on sunrise.

faq.q: How many people can join the sunrise fishing charter?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats – Astondoa 40 (max 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (max 11 pax) – are ideal for small groups. For larger parties up to 12 pax, we can upgrade to the Mangusta 80 (€4,719 minimum 4h) or other flybridge yachts. Contact us via WhatsApp for options.

faq.q: What fish can we catch on a Marbella sunrise charter?
faq.a: Typical catches include sea bass, bream, mackerel, and dentex. The Costa del Sol's waters are rich year-round. Your skipper knows the best spots off Cabopino and Cala del Faro. Catch-and-release is recommended, but you can keep a few for the grill.

faq.q: Is fishing gear included in the charter price?
faq.a: Yes, all rods, reels, bait, and tackle are provided. We supply spinning and bottom-fishing setups suitable for the local species. Just bring sunscreen, sunglasses, and a light jacket – it can be cool on the water at dawn.

faq.q: Can we eat the fish we catch?
faq.a: Absolutely. Your skipper can clean and fillet your catch. Many guests take it to a beach club or restaurant that will cook it for you. Otherwise, you're welcome to keep it in a cooler bag for later.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Your safety comes first. If the forecast shows strong winds or rough seas, we'll reschedule your charter at no extra cost or offer a full refund. We monitor conditions closely and confirm 24 hours before departure.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you're welcome to bring extra snacks or a picnic. Our standard charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No glass bottles, please – we provide plastic cups.

faq.q: Do we need a fishing license?
faq.a: No – your skipper holds the necessary permits for recreational fishing in Spanish waters. You just show up and fish. We follow local regulations, including size limits and catch quotas.

data.body_html:
There's something primal about casting a line as the sun lifts over La Concha. A sunrise fishing charter Marbella gets you on the water before the jet skis and day-trippers, when the sea is glassy and the fish are hungry. Departing from Puerto Banús, our 4-hour trip combines the thrill of the catch with the quiet beauty of dawn on the Costa del Sol. Licensed skipper, all gear, drinks, and snacks included – just bring your hat.

Why book a sunrise fishing charter in Marbella?

Fishing at first light isn't just about avoiding the heat (though that helps). Fish feed more actively in low light, and the lack of boat traffic means your bait gets noticed. Our skippers know the productive grounds off Cabopino, Cala del Faro, and the Río Verde mouth. Expect sea bass, bream, mackerel, and the occasional dentex. The 4-hour window – typically 06:30 to 10:30 – gives you prime feeding time and still leaves the rest of the day free for the beach.

Our boats for the dawn catch

We run the sunrise charter on our Tier A boats: the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax). Both are well-equipped with rod holders, a shaded cockpit, and a cooler for your catch. If you have a larger group (up to 12 pax), ask about the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge – pricing on request via WhatsApp. All charters include a licensed skipper who handles the navigation and knows the local marks.

What's included in the price

The €749/4h rate (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) covers everything: licensed skipper, fuel, all fishing gear and bait, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%). Onboard you'll find water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No hidden extras – the price is the price. For the Mangusta 80, the minimum spend is €4,719 for 4 hours (includes a jet ski).

Where we fish: best spots near Marbella

Our skippers rotate between proven locations depending on wind and tide. Common grounds include:

  • Off Cabopino – rocky bottoms holding bream and sea bass
  • Cala del Faro – deeper water for dentex and mackerel
  • Río Verde mouth – freshwater runoff attracts baitfish and predators
  • Estepona reefs – a longer run but worth it for bigger specimens

All within 30 minutes of Puerto Banús. Your skipper decides on the day based on conditions.

What to bring and wear

Dawn on the Mediterranean can be cool (15–18°C in spring/autumn), so a light windbreaker or fleece is smart. Sunscreen is essential even at 7am – the UV bounces off the water. Polarised sunglasses help you see into the water. No need for specialised gear; we supply everything. Leave the chunky heels at home – non-slip deck shoes or trainers are best.

Catch-and-release vs. keeping your catch

We encourage catch-and-release to preserve stocks, especially for larger bream and dentex. If you want to keep a few for the barbecue, your skipper will clean and fillet them. Many guests take their catch to a nearby chiringuito (beach bar) – try Los Sardinales in Cabopino – and they'll grill it for you. Please respect local size limits: sea bass under 36cm go back.

How to book your sunrise fishing charter

Reserve via our Boat Rental Marbella booking page or message us on WhatsApp. We need at least 48 hours' notice for the dawn trip to arrange bait and skipper. Payment is 50% deposit (secure via bank transfer or card), balance on the day. Cancellations due to weather are free – we'll reschedule or refund. For groups of 6+, we recommend the sunrise fishing charter Marbella experience page for full details.

Frequently asked questions

What time does a sunrise fishing charter in Marbella depart?

Departure is at first light, typically between 06:30 and 07:00 depending on the season. We meet at Puerto Banús 15 minutes before. The exact time is confirmed the day before based on sunrise.

How many people can join the sunrise fishing charter?

Our Tier A boats – Astondoa 40 (max 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (max 11 pax) – are ideal for small groups. For larger parties up to 12 pax, we can upgrade to the Mangusta 80 (€4,719 minimum 4h) or other flybridge yachts. Contact us via WhatsApp for options.

What fish can we catch on a Marbella sunrise charter?

Typical catches include sea bass, bream, mackerel, and dentex. The Costa del Sol's waters are rich year-round. Your skipper knows the best spots off Cabopino and Cala del Faro. Catch-and-release is recommended, but you can keep a few for the grill.

Is fishing gear included in the charter price?

Yes, all rods, reels, bait, and tackle are provided. We supply spinning and bottom-fishing setups suitable for the local species. Just bring sunscreen, sunglasses, and a light jacket – it can be cool on the water at dawn.

Can we eat the fish we catch?

Absolutely. Your skipper can clean and fillet your catch. Many guests take it to a beach club or restaurant that will cook it for you. Otherwise, you're welcome to keep it in a cooler bag for later.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Your safety comes first. If the forecast shows strong winds or rough seas, we'll reschedule your charter at no extra cost or offer a full refund. We monitor conditions closely and confirm 24 hours before departure.

Can we bring our own food and drinks?

Yes, you're welcome to bring extra snacks or a picnic. Our standard charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No glass bottles, please – we provide plastic cups.

Do we need a fishing license?

No – your skipper holds the necessary permits for recreational fishing in Spanish waters. You just show up and fish. We follow local regulations, including size limits and catch quotas.
